title: madhuca bejaudii
intro: Madhuca bejaudii is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, commonly known as the Bejaud's butter tree due to its large, edible fruit. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, with its extracts exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Madhuca bejaudii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in traditional medicine, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which can be applied topically to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
